title: The Man Behind AlphaGo Thinks AI Is Taking the Wrong Path
description: David Silver has a new billion-dollar company that aims to build AI “superlearners.”

title: Discord Sleuths Gained Unauthorized Access to Anthropic’s Mythos
description: Plus: Spy firms tap into a global telecom weakness to track targets, 500,000 UK health records go up for sale on Alibaba, Apple patches a revealing notification bug, and more.

title: Ace the Ping-Pong Robot Can Whup Your Ass
description: Ace can read the trajectory of a ball, adjust the racket angle, and respond with strokes that keep the exchange alive with real players.

title: AI-Designed Drugs by a DeepMind Spinoff Are Headed to Human Trials
description: Isomorphic Labs president Max Jaderberg said at WIRED Health in London that the startup has built a “broad and exciting pipeline of new medicines.”

title: Apple's Next CEO Needs to Launch a Killer AI Product
description: Tim Cook was a great CEO, but he didn’t crack AI.  It’s job number 1 for John Ternus.

title: These AI Thirst Trap Creators Say They’re Misunderstood
description: A viral red carpet moment shone light on a group of hunky Instagram influencers—and the followers who are too horny to care that they’re not real.

title: 5 Reasons to Think Twice Before Using ChatGPT—or Any Chatbot—for Financial Advice
description: As people increasingly rely on AI chatbots for guidance, even on financial matters, a healthy dose of skepticism is critical.

title: Apple’s Next Chapter, SpaceX and Cursor Strike a Deal, and Palantir’s Controversial Manifesto
description: In this week’s episode of Uncanny Valley, we talk about Tim Cook’s legacy as CEO at Apple and what his long-rumored departure means for the future of one of the world's biggest companies.

title: At 'AI Coachella,' Stanford Students Line Up to Learn From Silicon Valley Royalty
description: CS 153 has gone viral on the Palo Alto campus—and on X. Not everyone is happy about it.

title: Sam Altman’s Orb Company Promoted a Bruno Mars Partnership That Doesn't Exist
description: “To be clear, we were never approached … nor were we in any discussions regarding a partnership or tour access,” a spokesperson for the artist tells WIRED.
